questions.question: What is the contribution of Gems and jewellery to Indian GDP?
questions.explanation: The gems and jewelry plays an important role in the development of Indian economy. It contributes about 7% to Indian GDP. India is the leading exporter of processed diamonds in the world.

questions.question: What percentage do gems and jewellery contribute to India's merchandise exports?
questions.explanation: India's gems and jewellery exports reached $30 billion in 2024, representing about 12% of total merchandise exports, which were approximately $437-451 billion. For April-January 2025-26, gems and jewellery exports stood at $23.19 billion. The sector's share in India's merchandise exports has remained around 12% in recent years due to changes in export patterns and sectoral performance.

questions.question: What percentage of global rough diamonds are processed in India?
questions.explanation: India processes around 90% of the world's rough diamonds by volume. Surat, in Gujarat, is known as the major hub for diamond cutting and polishing in India. By 2024, India accounts for about 18.3% of global diamond export value. The diamond processing industry in India employs over one million people, primarily in Gujarat.

questions.question: Which country has the world's largest diamond cutting industry in the world?
questions.explanation: India has the world's largest diamond cutting and polishing centre in the world. It accounts for 95% of the world's processed diamonds.

questions.question: What is India's share in the global processed diamond market?
questions.explanation: India accounts for approximately 90% of the world's processed diamonds by volume. Surat in Gujarat is a major hub for diamond cutting and polishing. According to GJEPC, India's cut and polished diamond exports declined by 8.76% to $8.20 billion between April and November 2025 due to weak global demand and US tariffs. Diamonds are a principal export in India's gems and jewellery sector.
